The recent price action does not form a textbook example of any of the requested patterns.

fundamentals.name: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. engages in the design, manufacture, and sale of fiber-optic networking products in the United States, Taiwan, and China. The company offers optical modules, optical filters, lasers, laser components, subassemblies, transmitters and transceivers, turn-key equipment, headend, node, and distribution equipment, as well as amplifiers. It sells its products to internet data center operators, cable television, telecom equipment manufacturers, fiber-to-the-home, and internet service providers through its direct and indirect sales channels. Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. was incorporated in 1997 and is headquartered in Sugar Land, Texas.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue but currently loss-making with high valuation multiples and significant debt.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed estimates with EPS of -0.07 vs -0.05; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 51.4% YoY — exceptional top-line expansion.
Analyst growth est, current FY 441.7% — very high expected growth.
PEG ratio 0.78 — suggests growth may justify the forward P/E.
negatives: Operating margin -8.6% and Profit margin -8.5% — company is unprofitable.
Return on equity -6.1% and Return on assets -3.3% — poor returns on capital.
Forward P/E 38.99x — high multiple for an unprofitable company.
Free cash flow (TTM) $-445.58M — burning cash significantly.

news.paragraph: Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) is experiencing strong demand from data centers and AI computing, highlighted by a significant upgrade deal with Mediacom. However, the stock saw a notable dip recently, alongside other optics players, suggesting some cooling in the sector. Despite the volatility, analysts remain largely positive with a consensus 'buy' rating, though the mean target price is below the current trading level.
